data.content:
### Organize Registry Items with Category Tags in JSON Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/registry-item-json.mdx Assigns categories to registry items for organizational purposes. Categories help users find and filter components, allowing items to be tagged with multiple classifications such as sidebar, dashboard, or other relevant groupings. ```json { "categories": ["sidebar", "dashboard"] } ``` -------------------------------- ### Integrate ChartLegend into a shadcn-ui BarChart (TSX) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx Demonstrates how to add `ChartLegend` and `ChartLegendContent` within a `BarChart` component. This integration displays a legend, helping users understand the different data series represented in the chart, such as 'desktop' and 'mobile' data keys. ```tsx value.slice(0, 3)} /> } /> } /> ``` -------------------------------- ### Render a Dynamic Sidebar Menu in shadcn-ui (React/TSX) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/sidebar.mdx This snippet demonstrates how to populate a `SidebarMenu` with a dynamic list of items within a `SidebarGroup`. It iterates over a `projects` array, rendering each `project` as a `SidebarMenuItem`. Each menu item uses `SidebarMenuButton` with the `asChild` prop to render an anchor tag (``) linking to the project's URL, including an icon and name. ```tsx Projects {projects.map((project) => ( {project.name} ))} ``` -------------------------------- ### Define Chart Data for Recharts Bar Chart (TypeScript/TSX) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx This snippet defines the `chartData` array, representing monthly desktop and mobile user statistics. It serves as the primary data source for rendering a Recharts bar chart, where each object contains a `month`, `desktop`, and `mobile` property. ```tsx const chartData = [ { month: "January", desktop: 186, mobile: 80 }, { month: "February", desktop: 305, mobile: 200 }, { month: "March", desktop: 237, mobile: 120 }, { month: "April", desktop: 73, mobile: 190 }, { month: "May", desktop: 209, mobile: 130 }, { month: "June", desktop: 214, mobile: 140 }, ] ``` -------------------------------- ### Integrate ChartTooltip into a shadcn-ui Chart (TSX) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx Illustrates adding the `ChartTooltip` component to a chart, utilizing `ChartTooltipContent` for its rendering. This setup enables interactive tooltips that appear on hover, enhancing user experience by providing context-sensitive data. ```tsx } /> ``` -------------------------------- ### Install shadcn/ui Chart Component via CLI Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx This bash command uses the `npx shadcn@latest add chart` utility to quickly install the `chart.tsx` component into your project. This is the recommended method for integrating shadcn/ui components, simplifying the setup process. ```bash npx shadcn@latest add chart ``` -------------------------------- ### Fetch Sidebar Menu Data with React Query in React Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/sidebar.mdx Shows how to integrate React Query for fetching data to display in a `SidebarMenu`. This example demonstrates handling loading states with a skeleton component and mapping fetched project data to menu items, providing an alternative client-side data fetching strategy. ```tsx function NavProjects() { const { data, isLoading } = useQuery() if (isLoading) { return ( {Array.from({ length: 5 }).map((_, index) => ( ))} ) } if (!data) { return ... } return ( {data.map((project) => ( {project.name} ))} ) } ``` -------------------------------- ### Fetch Sidebar Menu Data with SWR in React Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/sidebar.mdx Illustrates how to populate a `SidebarMenu` with data fetched using the SWR library. It includes handling loading states with a skeleton component and rendering fetched project items, similar to the RSC approach but using a client-side data fetching library. ```tsx function NavProjects() { const { data, isLoading } = useSWR("/api/projects", fetcher) if (isLoading) { return ( {Array.from({ length: 5 }).map((_, index) => ( ))} ) } if (!data) { return ... } return ( {data.map((project) => ( {project.name} ))} ) } ``` -------------------------------- ### Apply Themed Colors with Tailwind CSS (TSX) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx Shows how to use Tailwind CSS utility classes to apply themed colors to components like `LabelList`, referencing CSS variables directly. This approach integrates shadcn-ui's theming with Tailwind for consistent styling. ```tsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Define a Chart Configuration Object in TypeScript Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx Illustrates the structure of a `chartConfig` object for shadcn-ui charts, used to define labels, icons, and color themes for different data keys. This configuration is decoupled from the chart data, allowing for shared styling and flexible data integration. ```tsx import { Monitor } from "lucide-react" import { type ChartConfig } from "@/components/ui/chart" const chartConfig = { desktop: { label: "Desktop", icon: Monitor, // A color like 'hsl(220, 98%, 61%)' or 'var(--color-name)' color: "#2563eb", // OR a theme object with 'light' and 'dark' keys theme: { light: "#2563eb", dark: "#dc2626", }, }, } satisfies ChartConfig ``` -------------------------------- ### Enable Accessibility Layer for Charts Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx Demonstrates how to add an accessibility layer to a chart component, such as `LineChart`, using the `accessibilityLayer` prop. This feature enhances keyboard navigation and screen reader support for the chart. ```tsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Render Sidebar Separator in shadcn/ui Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/sidebar.mdx Demonstrates how to integrate the `SidebarSeparator` component within a `Sidebar` to visually divide content. It shows typical placement within `Sidebar` and `SidebarContent` for structuring navigation elements. ```tsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Add Badge to SidebarMenuItem (TSX) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/sidebar.mdx This example illustrates how to integrate a `SidebarMenuBadge` within a `SidebarMenuItem`. The badge is typically used to display counts or notifications associated with the menu item, placed alongside the `SidebarMenuButton`. ```tsx 24 ``` -------------------------------- ### Implement Data Fetching for SidebarMenu with React Server Components Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/sidebar.mdx Demonstrates how to fetch data for a `SidebarMenu` using React Server Components, including a skeleton component for loading states and integration with React's `Suspense` for a better user experience during data retrieval. This pattern separates data fetching from client-side rendering concerns. ```tsx function NavProjectsSkeleton() { return ( {Array.from({ length: 5 }).map((_, index) => ( ))} ) } ``` ```tsx async function NavProjects() { const projects = await fetchProjects() return ( {projects.map((project) => ( {project.name} ))} ) } ``` ```tsx function AppSidebar() { return ( Projects }> ) } ``` -------------------------------- ### Implement Pagination Controls for React Table (TSX) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/data-table.mdx This snippet adds user interface controls for pagination to the `DataTable` component. It includes 'Previous' and 'Next' buttons, utilizing Shadcn UI's `Button` component, and connects them to the `table.previousPage()` and `table.nextPage()` methods, disabling buttons when no further pages are available. ```tsx import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button" export function DataTable({ columns, data, }: DataTableProps) { const table = useReactTable({ data, columns, getCoreRowModel: getCoreRowModel(), getPaginationRowModel: getPaginationRowModel(), }) return (
{ // .... }
) } ``` -------------------------------- ### Render SidebarMenu Loading Skeleton (TSX) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/sidebar.mdx This snippet demonstrates how to use `SidebarMenuSkeleton` to display a loading state for a `SidebarMenu`. It's particularly useful when fetching data with React Server Components, SWR, or react-query, providing a visual placeholder while content loads. ```tsx function NavProjectsSkeleton() { return ( {Array.from({ length: 5 }).map((_, index) => ( ))} ) } ``` -------------------------------- ### Provide Helper Text with FieldDescription in TSX Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/field.mdx The `FieldDescription` component offers a slot for helper text that automatically balances long lines, especially in horizontal layouts. It's used to provide additional context or guidance for a form field, improving user understanding without cluttering the main label. ```tsx We never share your email with anyone. ``` -------------------------------- ### Render Link as Badge using `asChild` prop (TSX) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/badge.mdx This function demonstrates how to combine a `Link` component (e.g., from Next.js) with the Shadcn UI `Badge` component using the `asChild` prop. The `asChild` prop ensures that the `Badge` component renders its child (`Link`) while applying its styling and accessibility attributes, making the link visually appear as a badge. ```tsx import Link from "next/link" import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge" export function LinkAsBadge() { return ( Badge ) } ``` -------------------------------- ### Apply Themed Colors within Chart Data (TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/chart.mdx Demonstrates how to embed CSS variable color references directly within the `chartData` array. This allows individual data points or series to inherit colors from the active theme, providing granular control over styling. ```tsx const chartData = [ { browser: "chrome", visitors: 275, fill: "var(--color-chrome)" }, { browser: "safari", visitors: 200, fill: "var(--color-safari)" }, ] ```
